hero.title: The System
hero.subtitle: A complete training doctrine built on one principle: The Human Is The Weapon.

doctrine.title: Core Doctrine
doctrine.description: BE THE WEAPON is not a training program. It is a system for developing human capability across all domains — physical, technical, and tactical. Every component is designed to build a complete fighter.

items.title: Strength
items.description: Functional strength through fundamental movements. Press. Deadlift. Squat. Not for aesthetics — for capability.
items.detail: The foundation of all physical performance. Without strength, speed has no impact. Without strength, endurance has no purpose.

items.title: Speed & Power
items.description: Acceleration, max velocity, change of direction. Athletic movement under load.
items.detail: Speed kills. Power ends fights. We train both through explosive movements, plyometrics, and sport-specific drills.

items.title: Aerobic Engine
items.description: Breathing control. Work capacity over time. The engine that keeps you operational.
items.detail: Your aerobic system determines how long you can operate. We build this engine through structured conditioning across all energy systems.

items.title: Durability
items.description: Mobility, joint stability, tendon resilience. Built to last.
items.detail: The most dangerous weapon is one that never breaks. Durability training ensures you remain operational through years of training and real-world application.

items.title: Combat Conditioning
items.description: Sandbags, carries, combat sports. Real load. Asymmetric pressure.
items.detail: Where physical capacity meets combat application. Loaded carries, sandbag work, and combat sport conditioning bridge the gap between the gym and the field.

pyramid.title: The Fighter Development Pyramid
pyramid.description: Every capability is built on the layer below. Skip a level, and everything above it collapses.

hero.title: Methodology
hero.subtitle: How we build fighters. The process behind the system.

gpp.title: General Physical Preparedness
gpp.description: Inspired by CrossFit methodology, adapted for tactical application. We develop across 10 physical skills:

conditioning.title: Strength & Conditioning Model
conditioning.gppDesc: Foundation for all. Broad physical development across all domains.
conditioning.sppDesc: Combat-specific conditioning. Targeted preparation for fighting demands.

types.title: Short MetCons
types.description: High intensity, short duration. Under 5 minutes. Maximum output.

types.title: Long MetCons
types.description: 20+ minutes. Aerobic capacity. Pacing and endurance.

hero.title: Training
hero.subtitle: The weekly cycle. Structure meets chaos.

structure.title: Weekly Training Structure
structure.description: Each week is designed to develop all components of the system. Different days target different capacities.

days.description: Heavy compound movements. Building raw power.
Unpredictable challenge. No preparation. Pure adaptation.

unknown.title: The Unknown Challenge
unknown.description: Every Friday, you face a challenge you didn't prepare for. Unknown format. Unknown duration. Maximum pressure.
unknown.purpose: This is where training becomes real. In life, you don't get to choose your challenges. Friday teaches you to perform when you can't prepare.
principles: Unknown workload — you never know what's coming
No controlled conditions — just like reality
Tests everything built during the week

hero.title: Standards
hero.subtitle: Measurable benchmarks. Clear levels. No guessing.

levels.title: Performance Levels
levels.description: Every fighter is measured against objective standards. Progress is tracked, not assumed.

tiers.name: Foundation
tiers.description: Basic physical competency. Entry-level standards for all components.

tiers.name: Operational
tiers.description: Combat-ready physical capacity. Able to perform under sustained load.

tiers.name: Advanced
tiers.description: High-performance standards. Exceptional across all domains.

tiers.name: Elite
tiers.description: Top-tier capability. Tactical-grade physical and mental performance.

hero.title: Intel
hero.subtitle: Knowledge base. Training insights. Field reports.

intel.coming: Intel reports coming soon. Training doctrine, performance analysis, and field notes.

hero.title: About
hero.subtitle: The origin. The mission. The standard.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: BE THE WEAPON was founded on one belief: The human body and mind, when properly trained, become the most effective weapon. We don't sell memberships. We build fighters.

location.title: Location
location.description: Our Combat Academy operates in Rishon LeZion. A raw, purpose-built training facility designed for serious preparation.

hero.title: Kids Program
hero.subtitle: Building capable young fighters. Discipline. Skill. Resilience.

intro.title: Training The Next Generation
intro.description: Our kids program adapts the BTW methodology for young athletes. We build physical capacity, teach combat skills, and develop mental toughness — all in a structured, age-appropriate environment.

items.title: Discipline
items.description: Structure, respect, and focus through martial arts training.

items.title: Physical Development
items.description: Strength, coordination, and athletic ability appropriate for their age.

items.title: Self-Defense
items.description: Real combat skills: grappling, striking fundamentals, situational awareness.

items.title: Mental Toughness
items.description: Learning to handle pressure, setbacks, and challenges with composure.

groups.name: Foundation
groups.description: Motor skills, basic martial arts, games-based training.

groups.name: Development
groups.description: Technical skills, light sparring, structured conditioning.

groups.name: Advanced
groups.description: Full combat training, competition prep, system integration.

cta.title: Enroll Your Child
cta.description: Give them the tools to be strong, capable, and resilient.

footer.tagline: Train the body. Develop the fighter. Be the weapon.
